title: OpenClaw v2026.5.4: Voice AI for Google Meet + WhatsApp Broadcast
description: OpenClaw v2026.5.4 ships Gemini-powered voice agents for Google Meet, WhatsApp Newsletter outbound, prompt-cache reuse, and 100+ reliability improvements for B2B SDR teams.
content:
# OpenClaw v2026.5.4: Voice AI for Google Meet + WhatsApp Broadcast

OpenClaw v2026.5.4 is out — and it's one of the biggest multi-channel updates of the year for B2B sales teams. This release lands **Gemini-powered voice AI for Google Meet**, **WhatsApp Newsletter/Channel outbound broadcast**, a **prompt-cache fix** that cuts API costs on long conversations, and over **100 bug fixes** across agents, plugins, and channels.

If your outbound sales stack runs on WhatsApp, Google Meet, Slack, or Telegram, read on.

---

## Highlight: Google Meet Voice Agent via Gemini Bridge

OpenClaw AI agents can now **speak in Google Meet** via Twilio dial-in, backed by the Gemini realtime voice bridge.

This isn't a simple TTS-over-a-call. v2026.5.4 delivers:

- **Paced audio streaming** — no more choppy delivery; audio is metered to match natural speech rhythm
- **Backpressure-aware buffering** — the queue is bounded and overloaded streams close before audio piles up
- **Barge-in queue clearing** — when a prospect interrupts, the queue clears instantly so the agent responds to what was *just* said
- **No TwiML fallback during active speech** — the realtime Gemini bridge stays active throughout, giving Meet participants a snappy, natural agent experience

For B2B SDR teams running **voice prospecting via Google Meet**, this changes what's possible: fully automated first-touch voice outreach, qualification calls, and follow-ups — all handled by an AI agent that speaks, listens, and adapts in realtime.

Paired with OpenClaw's existing `mode: "agent"` default for Google Meet (STT → OpenClaw agent → TTS), the agent now **forks the caller's current conversation transcript** into each meeting session, so voice replies inherit the full context of whatever CRM data, prior messages, or SDR instructions preceded the call.

> **Deploy PulseAgent with Google Meet voice outreach in 5 minutes:**

> curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iPythoning/b2b-sdr-agent-template/main/install.sh | bash

---

## WhatsApp Newsletter & Channel Outbound Broadcast

v2026.5.4 adds support for explicit **WhatsApp `@newsletter` outbound targets** — meaning your AI SDR agent can now send messages to WhatsApp Channels and Newsletter subscriber lists, not just individual contacts.

This is the missing piece for large-scale WhatsApp B2B outreach:

| Use case | Before v2026.5.4 | After v2026.5.4 |
|---|---|---|
| Individual DM outreach | Supported | Supported |
| Group chat outreach | Supported | Supported |
| Newsletter/Channel broadcast | Manual only | AI-driven |

For **manufacturing exporters, textile wholesalers, and electronics distributors** running WhatsApp as their primary buyer channel, this means product launches, seasonal promotions, and trade fair follow-ups can be dispatched automatically by an AI agent — at scale, with all the qualification and personalization logic you've already built.

See also: [WhatsApp Sales Automation for B2B Export →](/solutions/whatsapp-sales-automation)

---

## Prompt Cache Reuse Restored (Cost Impact)

This one slipped under the radar in v2026.5.3: per-turn runtime context was being injected into ordinary chat system prompts, breaking prompt-cache reuse on every continuation turn.

v2026.5.4 fixes it: **runtime context stays out of chat system prompts** while still being delivered as hidden current-turn context. For B2B SDR conversations that run 10–30 turns deep — which is normal for complex buyer qualification — this can meaningfully reduce API costs.

Expect 20–40% cache hit rate improvement on long-running SDR threads, depending on your conversation structure.

---

## Agent Performance: Workspace-Scoped Plugin Metadata

For teams running **multi-agent SDR deployments** — separate agents per channel, per product line, or per customer segment — v2026.5.4 reduces cold plugin metadata scans significantly.

The resolved workspace is now passed through:
- BTW (background task workers)
- Compaction
- Embedded-run model generation
- PDF model setup

Result: **agent-dir model refreshes reuse the current workspace-scoped plugin metadata snapshot** instead of triggering full cold scans. Combined with gateway startup improvements (deferred non-readiness sidecars, fast-path trusted bundled plugin metadata), this shaves meaningful latency off every cold start and context switch in containerized deployments.

---

## Slack: Rich Block Kit Progress Drafts

For teams using Slack as a **sales operations notification layer**, v2026.5.4 introduces `streaming.progress.render: "rich"` — Block Kit progress drafts backed by structured progress line data.

Instead of plain-text status lines like `Working on qualification...`, your Slack integration now renders structured cards with proper hierarchy, collapsible sections, and auto-trimming when Block Kit limits are hit. Internal SDR teams monitoring AI agent activity from Slack get a materially better experience.

---

## Key Bug Fixes for B2B SDR Workflows

**WhatsApp onboarding reliability**
Allowlist entries are now canonicalized to WhatsApp's digit-only phone IDs during setup, so E.164, JID, and `whatsapp:` format inputs reliably match WhatsApp Web sender IDs post-onboarding. Fewer "contact not matched" failures on first message.

**Docker/macOS workspace path fix**
Containerized deployments on macOS Docker no longer hit `EACCES: permission denied, mkdir '/Users'` — host-style workspace paths from `.env` are now properly pinned inside the container, not leaked into runtime config.

**Sub-agent long reports no longer lost**
In multi-step SDR flows using sub-agents for research, lead enrichment, or CRM lookups, long reports were sometimes silently truncated when the parent agent used prefix-only completion announces. This is fixed — full child results are now delivered.

**Active Memory bounded search query**
The memory recall sub-agent now uses a properly bounded search query. Channel/runtime metadata no longer bleeds into the memory search string, improving recall quality in long SDR threads.

**Discord IPv4 startup fix**
Discord REST and WebSocket gateway startup now prefer IPv4, fixing stalls on IPv4-only VPS and cloud environments that previously left the Discord channel stuck before `READY`.

**Telegram interactive reply buttons**
Plugin approval messages now correctly render inline keyboards in Telegram reply delivery. SDR approval workflows in Telegram work as expected.

---

## Security Improvements

v2026.5.4 ships multiple security hardening changes relevant for production SDR deployments:

- **SSRF**: Tab-scoped debug routes (screenshots, network traces, storage reads) now enforce the current-tab URL navigation policy *before* collection — blocked tabs return a policy error instead of being read and redacted post-facto
- **Windows**: Four separate fixes hardening `SystemRoot`, `WINDIR`, `LOCALAPPDATA`, and `ComSpec` resolution against workspace `.env` injection attacks
- **Exec approvals**: `env -S` split-string and POSIX `exec` carrier detection closes gaps in command risk assessment
- **QQBot**: Command authorization decision is now preserved when converting framework command contexts

---

## Upgrade

```bash
npx openclaw@latest update
# or
npm install -g openclaw@latest
```

No breaking config changes in v2026.5.4. Run `openclaw doctor --fix` after upgrading to apply any automated config migrations.

## FAQ

**Does Google Meet voice work without Twilio?**
The Gemini voice bridge currently uses Twilio for dial-in joins. Twilio credentials need to be configured. Direct WebRTC-based joins use `mode: "bidi"` and do not require Twilio.

**Does WhatsApp broadcast require a Business API account?**
Yes — WhatsApp Newsletter/Channel targets require a connected WhatsApp account with channel/newsletter owner permissions. Standard WhatsApp Business API accounts with channel management access qualify.

**Will v2026.5.4 work with my existing PulseAgent config?**
Yes. There are no breaking config changes. Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to apply automated migrations for Google Meet realtime provider config if you have existing Meet setups.

**How much will the prompt-cache fix save on API costs?**
It depends on your conversation depth and model provider. For conversations with 10+ turns using Claude or GPT-4, expect meaningful cache hit rate recovery — the exact saving depends on system prompt size and turn structure.
